notes: Editable categories now flow into the Journal too. WHAT WAS BROKEN: the Journal module built its expense-category list as a const (EXPENSE_CATS_FOR_TX) computed ONCE inside its IIFE at page load, so category renames, additions, and deletions made later in Edit-inputs never reached the Journal — its category picker (transaction modal) and its transaction labels kept showing the load-time snapshot. (The Budget page was already live because it calls getExpenseCats() on each render.) THE FIX: replaced the cached const with a live expenseCatsForTx() helper that recomputes from getExpenseCats() on every use — used by both the modal category chips and the journal transaction label. Now renamed labels, newly added custom categories, and removed categories immediately reflect in the Journal picker and list. ALSO: made the category label resolver robust for DELETED categories in both the Journal (labelForCat) and the Budget-page detail drilldown (_labelForCatLocal): if a transaction is tagged to a category that no longer exists, it now falls back to a rename override (state.categoryLabels) or the built-in default label (EXPENSE_CATEGORIES) instead of showing the raw key like 'exInsurance'; 'oneTime' and 'emi' resolve to friendly labels. Display-only; no engine/projection/savings math touched. KNOWN ITEM (still deferred): current-month savings forecast uses planned surplus. Runtime check still open: one-time expense debit correctness for credit-card/custom accounts. SELF_TEST.js and PROPAGATION_TEST_{1,2,3}.js remain valid.
